title: Alpha Release
description: Gate for making the repo public and sharing with coworkers. Language surface is honest — named arguments (#56), agents nest in modules (#63), types usable from agents (#70), enum variant construction completeness (#96), capability checks cover test blocks (#104) — schedule handlers fire on their own (#71), agent events survive crashes (#72), every spec example compiles or is marked Planned (#77), the first-five-minutes DX holds up (assertion failures say why #105, skein new git-inits #106, zsh completion #101, MCP compile_check tells the truth #109), and releases/badges/versioned docs are automated for public consumption (#100).

title: Beta Release
description: Post-alpha hardening, informed by alpha feedback: replay backend injection (#73), the capability-parameter surface decision (#69) and parameter-scoped enforcement for process/timer/event.log (#57), process.spawn task bodies (#74), enum value-level exhaustiveness warnings (#76), local LLM backends for dev via OpenAI-compatible servers + skein.toml profiles (#107), and LSP code actions from fix_hint/fix_code (#108). Triaged alpha bug reports land here unless they block alpha itself.

title: Post-MVP 1: Hardening & Language
description: First post-beta wave: fix what's broken and close the well-scoped language/runtime gaps. Bugs — Int interpolation emits raw codepoints (#114), queue/topic handlers never subscribed in running services (#121), flaky memory property test in CI (#118). Features — guard expressions in match arms (#147), an embeddings-capable LLM backend so llm.embed works for real (#146), llm.rerank for RAG (#145), and code-action phase 2 spans + edit_kind (#150).

title: Post-MVP 2: Interop & Agent Workflows
description: Bigger design efforts after the hardening wave: Erlang/Elixir FFI via extern (#141), human-in-the-loop approval workflows with suspend before sensitive tool calls (#144), and the browser-based trace viewer (#143).

title: Future: Platform
description: Deliberately deferred platform bets, parked until the core language and runtime stabilize: hot code upgrades (#142), the managed deployment platform (#148), and the tools/connectors marketplace (#149). Re-scope each from scratch when pulled into active work.
